## Purpur

[![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/PurpurMC/Purpur?&logo=github)](LICENSE)
[![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/PurpurMC/Purpur/build.yml?branch=ver%2F1.21.8&event=push&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://purpurmc.org/downloads/)

[![Join us on Discord](https://discord.com/api/guilds/685683385313919172/widget.png?style=shield)](https://purpurmc.org/discord)

[![Stargazers](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/PurpurMC/Purpur?label=stars&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://github.com/PurpurMC/Purpur/stargazers)
[![Forks](https://img.shields.io/github/forks/PurpurMC/Purpur?label=forks&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://github.com/PurpurMC/Purpur/network/members)
[![Watchers](https://img.shields.io/github/watchers/PurpurMC/Purpur?label=watchers&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://github.com/PurpurMC/Purpur/watchers)

Purpur is a drop-in replacement for [Paper](https://github.com/PaperMC/Paper) servers designed for configurability, new fun and exciting gameplay features, and performance built on top of [Paper](https://github.com/PaperMC/Paper/).

</div>

## Bonfire vs Purpur Current Delta

BonfireHearth is no longer just a branded Purpur copy. The current fork adds Bonfire-specific capacity and observability work aimed at model-heavy, MythicMobs-heavy production servers:

- Entity tracking budget for low-priority visual entities such as `Display`, `Interaction`, and marker-style armor stands
- Static living-entity sync budget for low-risk MM/model mobs, with pressure-driven metadata, equipment, and head-rotation throttling
- Idle mob AI budgeting for non-combat mobs without active targets
- Sync scheduler budgeting with plugin hotspot summaries and soft deferral under pressure
- Chunk send budgeting and chunk load/generation admission control
- Network pressure accounting for tracked entities, sync packets, estimated bytes, chunk sends, and unacknowledged chunk batches
- Low-memory monitoring focused on 2G-class pressure cases
- Bonfire-mode shutdown of vanilla natural spawning and listed vanilla special spawn paths
- Gray.4 network-pressure convergence with raw/effective split, hysteresis, and packet-kind breakdowns
- Forced runtime branding: `BonfireHearth Core` is now the only runtime brand source even if `purpur.yml` still contains a legacy `server-mod-name`

## Contact
Join us on Discord:

[![Join us on Discord](https://discord.com/api/guilds/685683385313919172/widget.png?style=banner2)](https://purpurmc.org/discord)

## Downloads
Downloads can be obtained from the [downloads page](https://purpurmc.org/downloads/) or the [downloads API](https://api.purpurmc.org).

[![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/PurpurMC/Purpur/build.yml?branch=ver%2F1.21.8&event=push&label=Downloads&logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://purpurmc.org/downloads/)

Downloads API endpoints:
 * List versions of Minecraft with builds available:
   `https://api.purpurmc.org/v2/purpur`
 * List builds for a version of Minecraft:
   `https://api.purpurmc.org/v2/purpur/<version>`
 * Download a specific build of a specific version:
   `https://api.purpurmc.org/v2/purpur/<version>/<build>/download`
 * Download the latest build for a version of Minecraft:
   `https://api.purpurmc.org/v2/purpur/<version>/latest/download`

## License
All patches are licensed under the MIT license, unless otherwise noted in the patch headers.

[![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/PurpurMC/Purpur?&logo=github)](LICENSE)

See [PaperMC/Paper](https://github.com/PaperMC/Paper), and [PaperMC/Paperweight](https://github.com/PaperMC/paperweight) for the license of material used by this project.

## API

### [Javadoc](https://purpurmc.org/javadoc)

### Dependency Information
Maven
```xml
<repository>
    <id>purpur</id>
    <url>https://repo.purpurmc.org/snapshots</url>
</repository>
```
```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.purpurmc.purpur</groupId>
    <artifactId>purpur-api</artifactId>
    <version>1.21.8-R0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <scope>provided</scope>
</dependency>
```

Gradle
```kotlin
repositories {
    maven("https://repo.purpurmc.org/snapshots")
}
```
```kotlin
dependencies {
    compileOnly("org.purpurmc.purpur:purpur-api:1.21.8-R0.1-SNAPSHOT")
}
```

Yes, this also includes all API provided by Paper, Spigot, and Bukkit.

## Building and setting up

#### Initial setup
First, <u>clone</u> this repository. Do not download it.

Then run the following command in the root directory:

```
./gradlew applyAllPatches
```

The project is now ready for use in your IDE.

#### Creating a patch

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md).

#### Compiling

Use the command `./gradlew build` to build the API and server. Compiled JARs
will be placed under `purpur-api/build/libs` and `purpur-server/build/libs`.
**These JARs are not used to start a server.**

To compile a server-ready purpurclip jar, run `./gradlew createMojmapBundlerJar`.
To install the `purpur-api` and `purpur` dependencies to your local Maven repo, run `./gradlew publishToMavenLocal`. The compiled purpurclip jar will be in `purpur-server/build/libs`.

### YourKit

![YourKit-Logo](https://www.yourkit.com/images/yklogo.png)

[YourKit](https://www.yourkit.com/), makers of the outstanding Java profiler,
support open source projects of all kinds with their full-featured [Java](https://www.yourkit.com/java/profiler)
and [.NET](https://www.yourkit.com/.net/profiler) application profilers. We thank them for allowing us to use their
software so we can make Purpur the best it can be.

[JetBrains](https://www.jetbrains.com/), creators of the IntelliJ IDEA, supports Purpur with one of their [Open Source Licenses](https://www.jetbrains.com/opensource/). IntelliJ IDEA is the recommended IDE for working with Purpur, and most of the Purpur team uses it.

## BonfireHearth Stage Log

### 2026-04-14 Stage: Gray log auto-analysis
- 新增 `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\analyze-bonfire-gray-log.ps1`，把炉心灰度日志中的 `Done`、调度摘要、探针结果、热点插件告警与关键内容警告自动提炼成报告。
- 已在真实灰度日志上生成 `bonfire-gray-analysis-20260414-213823.md`，并能自动回读探针 JSON 报告拿到完整计数，而不只依赖日志里露出的样本数。
- 这让炉心首版灰度从“能跑、能恢复”进一步推进到“能稳定复盘、能减少人工漏项”。

### 2026-04-14 Stage: Repeatable gray idle-soak runner
- 新增并验证 `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\run-bonfire-gray-idle-soak.ps1`，把炉心灰度的投放、启动、短时挂载、停服、恢复收成可重复执行脚本。
- 脚本已实测跑通，自动生成 `gray-idle-soak-summary-20260414-213823.md`，并确认测试后原 `purpur.yml` 恢复、临时灰度 jar 清理、`java` 进程退出。
- 这让后续炉心继续跑热区 / 探索类灰度时，不需要再手工拼接整套维护动作。

### 2026-04-14 Stage: Short idle stability validation
- 使用炉心灰度 jar 在真实正式服级插件栈下完成一轮短时稳定性运行，再次成功到达 `Done (63.140s)`。
- 启动后炉心调度摘要保持 `pressure=NORMAL`、`deferred=0`，没有出现新的核心级崩溃证据。
- 测试结束后已恢复原 `purpur.yml`、移除临时灰度 jar，并确认没有残留 `java` 进程。

### 2026-04-14 Stage: Validation probe compatibility scan
- 基于 `BonfireValidationProbe` 与 `BonfirePets` 报告完成首轮兼容扫描，确认炉心首版核心在真实重插件栈下可完成探针校验。
- `BetterModel` API / event bus 探针正常，当前没有看到炉心首版核心直接打坏模型接口的证据。
- 当前高量问题主要来自历史内容与迁移债务，例如 `player-missing-pet=1817`、`duplicate-pet-id=2`、`pet-without-category=5`，以及 `MythicMobs` 中大量旧写法残留。

### 2026-04-10 Stage: Core budget patches
- 完成 Java 21 兼容修复并固化 patch。
- 落地实体追踪预算、Mob AI 预算、同步调度任务预算。
- 落地区块发送预算、区块加载/生成准入预算。
- applyAllServerPatches 与 compileJava 已通过。

### 2026-04-11 Stage: Runtime quick wins round 1
- 在正式服运行目录下调 Spigot 实体 tracking/activation 范围。
- 调整 Paper collisions、markers tick、optimize-explosions、update-pathfinding-on-block-update。
- 目标是先做低风险削峰，压低展示实体、杂项实体与路径重算成本。

### 2026-04-12 Stage: Runtime quick wins round 2
- 关闭 ItemsAdder 和 ProtocolLib 的非必要 metrics/update-checker。
- 收紧 ItemsAdder 作物渲染频率、半径与单玩家显示上限。
- 关闭 MythicMobs 资源包自动启动生成，减少维护重启成本。

### 2026-04-12 Stage: MythicMobs spawning control
- 收紧 MythicMobs RandomSpawning 的全局与局部上限。
- 增大玩家簇合并距离，降低多人热区重复刷怪热点。
- 下调 MaxGenerationAttempts，减少高压时的无效生成尝试成本。


### 2026-04-12 Stage: ModelEngine tuning round 3
- 收紧 ModelEngine 的剔除频率、垂直渲染距离、背身/遮挡保留半径。
- 让远距离模型更早进入降频同步状态，继续压低模型同步开销。
- 目标是减少主城、活动区、宠物与展示模型密集区的无效更新。

### 2026-04-12 Stage: Release roadmap and validation checklist
- 建立炉心首个完整版本 TODO 路线。
- 建立灰度验证与观测清单，明确场景、指标与回退条件。
- 后续每个阶段完成后同步写入 README 阶段日志，保证 GitHub 项目记录完整。


### 2026-04-12 Stage: Scheduler observability upgrade
- 为 Bonfire 同步任务预算增加插件级 runtime/deferral 统计。
- 支持在高压与预算超限时输出 top runtime plugins 与 top deferred plugins。
- 目标是让首版灰度时能快速识别主线程调度热点插件，而不是只看到总卡顿。

## BonfireHearth First Release TODO

- [x] Java 21 兼容与 patch 体系修复
- [x] 实体追踪预算
- [x] Mob AI 预算
- [x] 同步调度任务预算
- [x] 区块发送预算
- [x] 区块加载/生成准入预算
- [x] 同步调度任务可观测化
- [ ] 继续补一条高价值核心优化线
- [ ] 形成核心默认推荐配置
- [ ] 形成兼容豁免清单
- [ ] 完成首轮灰度验证记录
- [ ] 整理首版发布与回滚说明
### 2026-04-12 Stage: World pressure diagnostics
- 为炉心核心增加高压世界摘要日志，按世界输出玩家数、实体数、装饰实体占比、top entity types 与 block entity ticker 数量。
- 挂点接入 MinecraftServer `tickChildren()` 收尾阶段，并在 ServerLevel 补充只读统计 accessor。
- `rebuildMinecraftSourcePatches` 通过，`minecraft source patches` 数量提升到 224；`compileJava` 通过。

### 2026-04-12 Stage: First release defaults and guardrails
- 新增 `BONFIRE_FIRST_RELEASE.md`，整理炉心首版默认参数基线。
- 明确 Display / ModelEngine 类视觉生态、AI 密集型生态、同步调度敏感插件、区块突发负载的兼容风险与调参顺序。
- 补齐首轮灰度验证清单与回滚顺序，让炉心首版具备可执行的上线手册。
### 2026-04-12 Stage: Gray-release record template
- 新增 `BONFIRE_GRAY_RELEASE_TEMPLATE.md`，为炉心首轮灰度验证提供固定记录格式。
- 让后续每次压测都能沉淀相同维度的数据：热区世界、热点实体类型、调度热点插件、可见回归与上线结论。
### 2026-04-14 Stage: Server-ready core artifact
- 炉心首个 `createMojmapBundlerJar` 产物已成功构建，server-ready bundler jar 已生成。
- 产物位于 `purpur-server/build/libs/purpur-bundler-1.21.8-R0.1-SNAPSHOT-mojmap.jar`。
- 已额外复制一份灰度交付副本到 `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\artifacts\BonfireHearth-1.21.8-rc1-mojmap.jar`，避免直接覆盖正式服核心。
### 2026-04-14 Stage: Gray deployment handoff
- 为首轮炉心灰度生成非破坏式替换脚本 `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\deploy-bonfire-hearth-gray.ps1`。
- 脚本会先备份当前正式服核心与关键配置，再把炉心灰度 jar 投放到服务器目录。
- 这一步不直接触碰运行中的正式服，只为首轮灰度切换提供可复用交付动作。
### 2026-04-14 Stage: Smoke startup validation
- 使用炉心首版 server-ready jar 对正式服同级插件栈执行启动烟雾验证，并成功到达 `Done (77.217s)`。
- 炉心同步任务预算与调度摘要日志已在真实重插件环境中开始输出，首批热点直接浮出：CustomCrops、MythicMobs、ItemsAdder、MCPets、ProtocolLib。
- 本轮验证后已恢复正式服 `purpur.yml` 并撤出测试 jar，保持运行目录处于可继续维护的安全状态。
### 2026-04-15 Stage: BonfireHearth Core rebrand and gray package
- Rebranded the visible runtime/server identity from `Purpur` to `BonfireHearth Core` while preserving `purpur.yml` compatibility.
- Updated startup, `Done (...)!`, `/version`, watchdog, manifest brand metadata, and gray version labeling for the first Bonfire-branded operator-facing build.
- Built the new gray artifact `purpur-bundler-1.21.8-R0.1-BONFIRE-GRAY.2-mojmap.jar` and copied the deployment handoff package to `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\artifacts\BonfireHearth-Core-1.21.8-gray.2-mojmap.jar`.
### 2026-04-15 Stage: Network pressure, low-memory mode, and vanilla spawning shutdown
- Added Bonfire network-pressure accounting for entity-sync packets/bytes, chunk-send packets, tracked entity sets, and unacknowledged chunk batches so crowded MM/model scenes are no longer judged by MSPT alone.
- Extended entity-sync budgeting into static low-risk living entities, preserving critical movement behavior while reducing metadata, equipment, and head-rotation churn in dense scenes.
- Added Bonfire low-memory monitoring and hard-disabled vanilla natural spawning state creation, natural spawn category filtering, and `PlayerNaturallySpawnCreaturesEvent` in Bonfire mode.
- Kept `/gamerule doMobSpawning` as a compatibility surface, but the command now emits a Bonfire compatibility notice because vanilla natural spawning is intentionally ignored.
- Closed Bonfire-mode vanilla special spawn paths for skeleton horse thunder traps, cat spawning, wandering trader checks, zombie reinforcements, portal piglins, and creaking-heart protector spawning.
- Promoted the next gray build line to `1.21.8-R0.1-BONFIRE-GRAY.3`.
### 2026-04-15 Stage: Gray.4 branding and network-pressure convergence
- Forced `BonfireHearth Core` to become the only runtime branding source in the fork, even when legacy `purpur.yml` files still contain `server-mod-name: Purpur`.
- Split Bonfire network pressure into raw diagnostics and effective throttle pressure, then added hysteresis so dense-MM throttling no longer reacts to single noisy ticks.
- Added packet-kind accounting for metadata, equipment, head rotation, movement, and chunk traffic to help confirm whether ModelEngine / MEG ecosystems are bandwidth-heavy because of sync churn instead of MSPT alone.
- Tightened static living-entity sync eligibility to `required-stationary-ticks=20` and `max-stationary-velocity-sqr=0.0004` so Gray.4 can keep the `120+` crowd gains while reducing low-count false positives.
- Updated the Gray.4 operator handoff files so the runtime config, artifact notes, and stage history all reflect the same branding and network-pressure model.
### 2026-04-15 Stage: Gray.4 compile recovery
- Closed the remaining Paper/Purpur API drift that was blocking BonfireHearth from compiling on the current Java 21 toolchain and 1.21.8 upstream state.
- Restored missing bridge methods for Bonfire/Purpur additions across server branding, offline-player controls, rider APIs, item immunities, entity-specific Purpur hooks, debug markers, and scheduler/network-related compatibility surfaces.
- Verified the recovery with `:purpur-server:compileJava -x :purpur-server:downloadMcLibrariesSources`, which now completes successfully again on the BonfireHearth Gray.4 source tree.
- This stage re-establishes a buildable Gray.4 baseline so the next step can move forward to server-ready jar packaging instead of staying stuck in worktree-only compile breakage.
### 2026-04-15 Stage: Gray.4 server-ready artifact rebuild
- Rebuilt the runnable Gray.4 server artifact after the compile-recovery pass with `:purpur-server:createMojmapBundlerJar -x :purpur-server:downloadMcLibrariesSources`.
- Produced the fresh source artifact `purpur-bundler-1.21.8-R0.1-BONFIRE-GRAY.4-mojmap.jar` from the recovered BonfireHearth source tree.
- Refreshed the gray handoff copy at `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\artifacts\BonfireHearth-Core-1.21.8-gray.4-mojmap.jar` so the deployable package matches the compile-fixed Gray.4 codebase instead of the older pre-recovery output.
### 2026-04-15 Stage: Gray.4 startup hotfix
- Diagnosed the Gray.4 startup failure from the live server log and confirmed the crash was a core bootstrap bug instead of a plugin/config-content failure.
- Fixed `org.bukkit.craftbukkit.Main` so BonfireHearth once again registers the `purpur` / `purpur-settings` startup option and feeds a non-null `purpur.yml` path into the legacy Purpur config loader.
- Rebuilt the runnable Gray.4 mojmap bundler jar after the startup fix so operators can replace the broken first Gray.4 handoff without waiting for the next gray channel.
- This hotfix exists specifically because the earlier `BonfireHearth-Core-1.21.8-gray.4-mojmap.jar` package can fail at startup with `PaperConfigurations.loadLegacyConfigFile(...): configFile is null`.
### 2026-04-15 Stage: Gray.4 plugin-compatibility hotfix
- Diagnosed the next Gray.4 failure wave and confirmed that multiple plugins were not independently regressing; they were all losing the Bukkit version metadata required by their shared version-detection layers.
- Restored `Versioning.getBukkitVersion()` compatibility by probing both the legacy `paper-api` Maven metadata path and the current `purpur-api` metadata path before falling back to `Unknown-Version`.
- Updated the `purpur-api` jar packaging so the generated `pom.properties` is now written to all three compatibility locations: `org.purpurmc.purpur/purpur-api`, `io.papermc.paper/paper-api`, and `com.destroystokyo.paper/paper-api`.
- This hotfix specifically targets Gray.4 plugin stacks such as ItemsAdder, CMILib, MythicLib, and other version-sensitive Paper/Purpur ecosystem plugins that can fail when Bukkit version metadata is missing.
### 2026-04-16 Stage: Gray.4 hotfix3 build convergence
- Recovered the active BonfireHearth worktree back to a fully buildable Java 21 state by closing the remaining Paper/Purpur bridge drift directly in the current `paper-server` sources.
- Restored the last missing compatibility surfaces for server name, lagging state, AFK hooks, Purpur-client detection, item immunities, entity-specific Purpur APIs, and explorer/debug marker bridges that were still blocking the Gray.4 line.
- Re-verified the recovered source tree with `:purpur-server:compileJava -x :purpur-server:downloadMcLibrariesSources` and rebuilt the runnable server artifact with `:purpur-server:createMojmapBundlerJar -x :purpur-server:downloadMcLibrariesSources`.
- Refreshed the operator handoff package as `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\artifacts\BonfireHearth-Core-1.21.8-gray.4-hotfix3-mojmap.jar` so further Gray.4 validation can target this compile-converged build instead of the older handoff jars.
### 2026-04-16 Stage: Gray.4 hotfix4 runtime wiring
- Wired Bonfire startup guard and low-memory monitoring into the actual server lifecycle so Gray.4 now records startup readiness at `Done (...)!`, tracks warmup/join-protect during live ticks, and emits Bonfire startup summaries at boot.
- Hard-cut the vanilla natural spawning main path in `ServerChunkCache`: Bonfire mode now skips `NaturalSpawner.createState(...)`, prevents natural spawn category collection, and only allows custom spawners through the Bonfire gate.
- Connected Bonfire vanilla-entity cleanup into real runtime paths: entity-add now filters or queues unwanted vanilla mobs, and world ticks now consume the cleanup queue under Bonfire startup/cleanup budgets instead of leaving the cleanup subsystem idle.
- Gated vanilla special spawn entry points including thunder trap horses, zombie reinforcements, cat/wandering trader/patrol/phantom/village siege spawners, portal piglins, ender-pearl endermites, and creaking-heart protectors behind Bonfire spawning rules.
- Rebuilt the runnable gray handoff as `Z:\文档\炉心灰度\artifacts\BonfireHearth-Core-1.21.8-gray.4-hotfix4-mojmap.jar` after verifying `:purpur-server:compileJava` and `:purpur-server:createMojmapBundlerJar` both pass on the newly wired runtime.
